skill.name: service-mesh-observability
skill.description: Implement comprehensive observability for service meshes including distributed tracing, metrics, and visualization. Use when setting up mesh monitoring, debugging latency issues, or implementing SLOs.
skill.summary: Complete guide to observability patterns for Istio, Linkerd, and service mesh deployments with tracing, metrics, and alerting.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation-only guide for service mesh observability. Static analysis flagged 55 patterns, but all are false positives: backtick commands are markdown code blocks (not execution), hardcoded URLs/IPs are configuration examples, and crypto warnings are triggered by YAML config snippets. No actual code execution, network calls, or filesystem operations occur.

content.value_statement: Set up comprehensive monitoring, tracing, and alerting for your service mesh deployments. Get ready-to-use configurations for Istio, Linkerd, Prometheus, Grafana, and Jaeger.
actual_capabilities: Generate Istio and Linkerd observability configurations
Create Prometheus scrape configs and ServiceMonitors
Build Grafana dashboard JSON for mesh metrics
Set up distributed tracing with Jaeger or Zipkin
Define alerting rules for golden signals
Configure Kiali for service topology visualization
limitations: Provides configuration templates only - does not deploy resources
Requires existing Kubernetes cluster with service mesh installed
Does not replace dedicated observability platform expertise
Configuration examples may need adaptation for specific environments

use_cases.title: Platform Engineer Setting Up Mesh Monitoring
use_cases.description: Deploy complete observability stack for new Istio installation with Prometheus, Grafana, and Jaeger integration.

use_cases.title: SRE Debugging Production Latency Issues
use_cases.description: Query distributed traces to identify bottlenecks across microservices and set up P99 latency alerts.

use_cases.title: DevOps Team Implementing SLOs
use_cases.description: Define and monitor service level objectives for mesh traffic with automated alerting on error rate thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Mesh Metrics Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Prometheus ServiceMonitor configuration for scraping Istio mesh metrics with 15 second intervals.

prompt_templates.title: Distributed Tracing Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Jaeger deployment manifest for Istio tracing with 100 percent sampling for development environments.

prompt_templates.title: Grafana Dashboard Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Grafana dashboard JSON with panels for request rate, error rate, P99 latency, and service topology for Istio.

prompt_templates.title: Production Alerting Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Write PrometheusRule alerts for high error rate above 5 percent and P99 latency over 1 second with appropriate severity labels.

output_examples.input: Generate Prometheus config for Istio metrics
output_examples.output: YAML ServiceMonitor with scrape configs targeting istiod endpoints, 15 second intervals, and relabel rules for mesh discovery.
PrometheusRule with histogram_quantile expression for P99 latency threshold, 5 minute evaluation window, and warning severity annotation.

best_practices: Sample traces at 100 percent in development but reduce to 1-10 percent in production to control storage costs
Configure alerting on golden signals: latency, traffic, errors, and saturation with appropriate thresholds
Use trace context propagation consistently across all services for complete request visibility
anti_patterns: Over-sampling traces in production leading to excessive storage costs and performance overhead
Ignoring metric cardinality limits causing Prometheus memory issues and slow queries
Deploying observability tools without dashboards or alerts that provide actionable insights

faq.question: What service meshes does this skill support?
faq.answer: Primary support for Istio and Linkerd with configurations for Prometheus, Grafana, Jaeger, Kiali, and OpenTelemetry integration.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy resources to my cluster?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates configuration templates and manifests that you review and apply manually with kubectl apply.

faq.question: What sampling rate should I use for tracing?
faq.answer: Use 100 percent in development for complete visibility. In production, start with 10 percent and adjust based on traffic volume and storage budget.

faq.question: Can I use this with managed service meshes?
faq.answer: Yes. Configurations work with managed Istio (GKE, AKS, EKS) and Linkerd, though some installation steps may differ from self-managed deployments.

faq.question: How do I correlate metrics with traces?
faq.answer: Use Prometheus exemplars to link metric datapoints to trace spans. Configure your tracing backend to expose trace IDs in metric metadata.

faq.question: What are the golden signals for service mesh?
faq.answer: Latency (request duration), Traffic (requests per second), Errors (5xx rate), and Saturation (resource utilization). Alert on anomalies in these metrics.
